docs.title: SPIMemory
docs.text:
SPIMemory is an Arduino library for SPI Flash and SPI FRAM chips.

This manual is organized for:

  • firmware users integrating the library into applications
  • maintainers extending compatibility and behavior

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text:
• Use Quick Start to wire, initialize, and perform first read/write.
  • Use Troubleshooting when chips are non-responsive.
  • Use SPIFlash API and SPIFram API for the reference surface.

docs.title: Project Links
docs.text:
• Repository: https://github.com/Marzogh/SPIMemory
  • Published docs: https://chipsncode.com/SPIMemory/

docs.title: Page template
docs.text:
Each page should include:

  1. Purpose
  2. When to use
  3. Minimal examples
  4. Pitfalls / failure modes

docs.title: Writing rules
docs.text:
• Prefer short, direct paragraphs.
  • Keep one canonical page per API topic.
  • Avoid duplicate function declarations across pages.
  • Use fenced code blocks with language tags.

docs.title: Link policy
docs.text:
• Use absolute URLs for external links.
  • Use relative links for internal docs links.
  • Prefer canonical docs URL https://chipsncode.com/SPIMemory/ when sharing.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
Run before submitting:

mkdocs build --strict

docs.title: Legacy Documentation
docs.text:
Legacy Sphinx/RST documentation remains in the repository for historical reference.

Use these as archival material while migration to the new docs system continues.

docs.title: Legacy source locations
docs.text:
• docs/pages/SPIFlash/*.rst
  • docs/pages/info/*.rst
  • docs/pages/contribute.rst
  • docs/pages/license.rst

docs.title: Legacy links
docs.text:
• Old installation page source: https://github.com/Marzogh/SPIMemory/blob/master/docs/pages/info/installation.rst
  • Old SPIFlash index source: https://github.com/Marzogh/SPIMemory/blob/master/docs/pages/SPIFlash/index.rst

docs.title: Local setup
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt

docs.title: Local preview
docs.text: mkdocs serve

docs.title: Production build
docs.text:
mkdocs build --strict

The generated site is written to site/ and should only be published from CI to gh-pages.

docs.title: Docs Release Checklist
docs.text:
1. mkdocs build --strict succeeds locally.
  2. Navigation includes all new/changed pages.
  3. No broken internal links.
  4. CI docs-check workflow passes.
  5. Published site reflects merged commit.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
This section is the authoritative API surface summary.

Each function entry should describe:

  • purpose
  • preconditions
  • return semantics
  • side effects
  • failure behavior

docs.title: Fast Navigation
docs.text:
• SPIFlash function-by-function reference
  • SPIFram function-by-function reference
  • Error handling and diagnostics

docs.title: Retrieval
docs.text:
• error() returns the last error code.
  • error(VERBOSE) prints diagnostic context.

docs.title: Error classes
docs.text:
• initialization/state errors
  • address/boundary errors
  • write-enable and busy-state errors
  • verification failures
  • unsupported hardware/function errors

docs.title: Operational guidance
docs.text: Capture and map key error codes into firmware telemetry for faster field debugging.

docs.title: SPIFlash Function Reference
docs.text: This page lists every public SPIFlash call with beginner-focused usage notes.

docs.title: SPIFlash(uint8_t cs = CS)
docs.text: Use this on most boards with default SPI pins and a chosen chip-select pin.

docs.title: SPIFlash(uint8_t cs, SPIClass *spiinterface)
docs.text: Use this on boards with multiple SPI buses (for example SPI1, SPI2).

docs.title: SPIFlash(int8_t *SPIPinsArray)
docs.text: ESP32-style custom SPI pins constructor (sck, miso, mosi, ss).

docs.title: bool begin(uint32_t flashChipSize = 0)
docs.text: Initialize SPI and identify the chip. Call once in setup().

docs.title: void setClock(uint32_t clockSpeed)
docs.text: Set SPI bus speed (transaction-capable cores).

docs.title: bool libver(uint8_t *b1, uint8_t *b2, uint8_t *b3)
docs.text: Read library semantic version components.

docs.title: bool sfdpPresent(void)
docs.text: Returns whether SFDP information is available.

docs.title: uint8_t error(bool verbosity = false)
docs.text: Returns last library error code. Use verbosity=true to print details.

docs.title: uint16_t getManID(void)
docs.text: Get manufacturer ID.

docs.title: uint32_t getJEDECID(void)
docs.text: Get JEDEC ID (manufacturer + type + capacity ID bytes).

docs.title: uint64_t getUniqueID(void)
docs.text: Read unique chip ID when supported.

docs.title: uint32_t getCapacity(void)
docs.text: Returns chip capacity in bytes.

docs.title: uint32_t getMaxPage(void)
docs.text: Returns max page count using internal page-size configuration.

docs.title: float functionRunTime(void)
docs.text: Returns runtime for the previous library call (diagnostic mode use).

docs.title: uint32_t getAddress(uint16_t size)
docs.text: Find next free region for a block of size bytes.

docs.title: uint16_t sizeofStr(String &inputStr)
docs.text: Return the library storage size needed for a String.

docs.title: bool writeByte(uint32_t _addr, uint8_t data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one byte.

docs.title: uint8_t readByte(uint32_t _addr, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one byte.

docs.title: bool writeChar(uint32_t _addr, int8_t data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one signed 8-bit value.

docs.title: int8_t readChar(uint32_t _addr, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one signed 8-bit value.

docs.title: bool writeShort(uint32_t _addr, int16_t data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one signed 16-bit value.

docs.title: int16_t readShort(uint32_t _addr, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one signed 16-bit value.

docs.title: bool writeWord(uint32_t _addr, uint16_t data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one unsigned 16-bit value.

docs.title: uint16_t readWord(uint32_t _addr, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one unsigned 16-bit value.

docs.title: bool writeLong(uint32_t _addr, int32_t data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one signed 32-bit value.

docs.title: int32_t readLong(uint32_t _addr, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one signed 32-bit value.

docs.title: bool writeULong(uint32_t _addr, uint32_t data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one unsigned 32-bit value.

docs.title: uint32_t readULong(uint32_t _addr, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one unsigned 32-bit value.

docs.title: bool writeFloat(uint32_t _addr, float data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one float.

docs.title: float readFloat(uint32_t _addr, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one float.

docs.title: bool writeStr(uint32_t _addr, String &data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write one String.

docs.title: bool readStr(uint32_t _addr, String &data, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read one String.

docs.title: bool writeByteArray(uint32_t _addr, uint8_t *data_buffer, size_t bufferSize, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write a byte buffer.

docs.title: bool readByteArray(uint32_t _addr, uint8_t *data_buffer, size_t bufferSize, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read a byte buffer.

docs.title: bool writeCharArray(uint32_t _addr, char *data_buffer, size_t bufferSize, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write a char buffer.

docs.title: bool readCharArray(uint32_t _addr, char *data_buffer, size_t buffer_size, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read a char buffer.

docs.title: template <class T> bool writeAnything(uint32_t _addr, const T& data, bool errorCheck = true)
docs.text: Write struct/custom type bytes as stored in memory.

docs.title: template <class T> bool readAnything(uint32_t _addr, T& data, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read struct/custom type bytes into an object.

docs.title: bool eraseSection(uint32_t _addr, uint32_t _sz)
docs.text: Erase enough sectors/blocks for a region size.

docs.title: bool eraseSector(uint32_t _addr)
docs.text: Erase one 4KB sector containing address.

docs.title: bool eraseBlock32K(uint32_t _addr)
docs.text: Erase one 32KB block containing address.

docs.title: bool eraseBlock64K(uint32_t _addr)
docs.text: Erase one 64KB block containing address.

docs.title: bool eraseChip(void)
docs.text: Erase the entire flash chip.

docs.title: bool suspendProg(void)
docs.text: Suspend eligible erase/program operations.

docs.title: bool resumeProg(void)
docs.text: Resume a suspended operation.

docs.title: bool powerDown(void)
docs.text: Put chip in low-power mode.

docs.title: bool powerUp(void)
docs.text: Wake chip from power-down mode.

docs.title: Beginner Notes
docs.text:
• Flash usually requires erase-before-write.
  • Start with default errorCheck=true.
  • Enable RUNDIAGNOSTIC during bring-up.

docs.title: SPIFlash API
docs.text: For full per-call detail, use the SPIFlash Function Reference.

docs.title: Functional groups
docs.text:
• Initialization: begin, setClock, sfdpPresent
  • Identification: getJEDECID, getUniqueID, getCapacity, getMaxPage
  • Addressing helpers: getAddress, sizeofStr
  • Read/write primitives and arrays
  • Generic IO: writeAnything, readAnything
  • Erase operations: sector/block/chip/section
  • Power and suspend/resume

docs.title: Behavioral notes
docs.text:
• Writes are page-aware and support page-boundary spanning.
  • Write verification is enabled by default.
  • Erase timing comes from defaults or SFDP when enabled.

docs.text: This page lists every public SPIFram call with beginner-focused usage notes.

docs.title: SPIFram(uint8_t cs = CS)
docs.text: Default constructor for standard SPI bus.

docs.title: SPIFram(uint8_t cs, SPIClass *spiinterface)

docs.title: bool begin(uint32_t flashChipSize = 0)
docs.text: Initialize and identify FRAM device.

docs.title: void setClock(uint32_t clockSpeed)
docs.text: Set SPI clock speed.

docs.title: bool libver(uint8_t *b1, uint8_t *b2, uint8_t *b3)
docs.text: Read FRAM library version components.

docs.title: uint8_t error(bool verbosity = false)
docs.text: Get last error code; optional verbose print.

docs.title: uint16_t getManID(void)
docs.text: Get manufacturer ID.

docs.title: uint32_t getJEDECID(void)
docs.text: Get JEDEC ID.

docs.title: uint64_t getUniqueID(void)
docs.text: Get unique FRAM serial ID when supported.

docs.title: uint32_t getAddress(uint16_t size)
docs.text: Find next free region for a block size.

docs.title: uint16_t sizeofStr(String &inputStr)
docs.text: Compute String storage size helper.

docs.title: uint32_t getCapacity(void)
docs.text: Get FRAM capacity in bytes.

docs.title: float functionRunTime(void)
docs.text: Get runtime of previous operation.

docs.text: Write custom type bytes.

docs.title: template <class T> bool readAnything(uint32_t _addr, T& data, bool fastRead = false)
docs.text: Read custom type bytes.

docs.title: bool eraseSection(uint32_t _addr, uint32_t _sz)
docs.text: Clear a specific region.

docs.title: bool eraseChip(void)
docs.text: Clear entire FRAM.

docs.title: bool powerDown(void)
docs.text: Enter low-power mode.

docs.title: bool powerUp(void)
docs.text: Exit low-power mode.

docs.title: Beginner Notes
docs.text:
• FRAM does not require flash-style sector erase before each write.
  • Keep errorCheck=true during development.
  • Validate behavior on your exact FRAM chip model.

docs.title: SPIFram API
docs.text: For full per-call detail, use the SPIFram Function Reference.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: FRAM support exists in the library and should be treated as a narrower compatibility surface than SPIFlash.

docs.title: Functional groups
docs.text:
• Initialization and identification
  • Primitive and typed read/write
  • Region/chip clear operations
  • Power control

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• FRAM write semantics differ from flash (no erase-before-write requirement).
  • Validate behavior on your exact FRAM part number.

docs.title: Save one number and read it back
docs.text: Use writeULong and readULong.

docs.title: Save a text string
docs.text: Use writeStr and readStr.

docs.title: Save a custom struct
docs.text: Use writeAnything and readAnything.

docs.title: Find where to write next
docs.text: Use getAddress(size) before writing.

docs.title: Erase enough flash for a variable-sized record
docs.text: Use eraseSection(address, size).

docs.title: Chip not responding
docs.text:
1. Confirm wiring and CS pin.
  2. Call begin() in setup().
  3. Read error(VERBOSE).
  4. Enable RUNDIAGNOSTIC during debug.

docs.title: Speed up reads/writes
docs.text:
• Reads: use fastRead=true where needed.
  • Writes: disable error check only after validation.

docs.title: Compatibility Matrix
docs.text: Use this page as the canonical compatibility source for current releases.

docs.title: Recommended table fields
docs.text:
• Board / MCU
  • Core package version
  • SPI mode used (default or alternate)
  • Flash chips validated
  • FRAM chips validated
  • Known caveats

docs.title: Maintenance rule
docs.text:
Any new support claim should include:

  • exact board and core version tested
  • exact chip part number
  • required compile-time flags

docs.title: Addressing
docs.text: All APIs use a 32-bit linear address model.

docs.title: Erase Before Write (Flash)
docs.text: Flash writes require erased memory (typically 0xFF). Use erase APIs before writing to previously used regions.

docs.title: Overflow
docs.text: By default, operations can roll over from end-of-chip back to 0x00. Define DISABLEOVERFLOW to make out-of-bounds fail instead.

docs.title: Performance and Safety
docs.text:
• fastRead=true can speed reads on supported chips.
  • Write functions validate data by default; disabling checks increases speed and risk.

docs.title: Flash vs FRAM
docs.text:
• Flash: erase required, generally larger capacity.
  • FRAM: no erase needed for byte writes, lower write latency.

docs.title: Compile-Time Flags (in SPIMemory.h)
docs.text:
• USES_SFDP: enable SFDP discovery for unsupported flash chips
  • RUNDIAGNOSTIC: verbose diagnostics output
  • HIGHSPEED: skip pre-write blank checks
  • DISABLEOVERFLOW: disable address rollover
  • ENABLEZERODMA: experimental SAMD DMA mode

docs.title: Runtime Configuration
docs.text:
• begin(chipSize) optionally forces chip size
  • setClock(clockSpeed) changes SPI clock settings
  • platform-specific constructors enable alternate SPI interfaces

docs.title: Platform Notes
docs.text: When default SPI pins/bus are unsuitable, use board-specific constructor variants.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Initialize a flash chip and verify one write/read cycle.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• Wire SPI pins correctly (SCK, MISO, MOSI, CS, VCC, GND)
  • Instantiate SPIFlash with the desired chip-select pin
  • Call begin() in setup()
  • Erase the target region before first flash write

docs.title: Minimal Sketch
docs.text:
#include <SPIMemory.h>

SPIFlash flash(10);

void setup() {
  Serial.begin(115200);
  flash.begin();

  const uint32_t addr = 0;
  flash.eraseSector(addr);
  flash.writeULong(addr, 0xDEADBEEF);

  uint32_t out = flash.readULong(addr);
  Serial.println(out, HEX);
}

void loop() {}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Use getAddress() for automatic allocation.
  • Use typed APIs and writeAnything/readAnything for structs.
  • Enable diagnostics during bring-up and hardware debug.

docs.title: Critical code paths
docs.text:
• SPIFlash::_prep and _addressCheck
  • SPIFlash::_chipID and SFDP branch
  • architecture-specific SPI transaction start/end
  • FRAM identification and capacity derivation

docs.title: Adding chip support
docs.text:
• update ID/capacity detection
  • define erase/program behavior and timing
  • validate read/write/erase/power flows on hardware
  • update docs and examples in same change

docs.title: Regression focus
docs.text:
• page-boundary writes
  • overflow behavior
  • suspend/resume semantics
  • power-down/power-up semantics

docs.title: Docs
docs.text:
• compatibility matrix updated
  • API reference updated for all changed methods
  • docs build passes in CI

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• compile examples across representative boards
  • verify read/write/erase on at least one flash and one FRAM part
  • verify diagnostics still map correctly

docs.title: Publish
docs.text:
• merge to main branch
  • confirm docs publish workflow succeeded
  • verify site URL and key links

docs.title: Chip does not respond
docs.text:
• verify wiring and voltage levels
  • verify CS pin selection
  • lower SPI clock and retest
  • ensure begin() is called before IO

docs.title: Write failures
docs.text:
• erase flash region before writing
  • disable HIGHSPEED while debugging
  • check address boundaries and overflow configuration

docs.title: Intermittent behavior
docs.text:
• verify shared SPI bus ownership
  • verify board-core transaction compatibility
  • enable diagnostics and capture error code sequence
